examples: he's in a sweat about exams

meanings.definition: a spot that has been worn away by abrasion or erosion
an ornamental pattern consisting of repeated vertical and horizontal lines (often in relief)
examples: there was a simple fret at the top of the walls

meanings.definition: a small bar of metal across the fingerboard of a musical instrument; when the string is stopped by a finger at the metal bar it will produce a note of the desired pitch

examples: don't fuss too much over the grandchildren--they are quite big now
don't fret over these small details

meanings.definition: become or make sore by or as if by rubbing
gnaw into; make resentful or angry
be too tight; rub or press
examples: This neckband is choking the cat
